title: AI War Cloud Database
venue_prize: STARTS Prize 2025 Grand Prize for Artistic Exploration; Ars Electronica Festival, Linz, 3–7 Sept 2025
decisive_move: Maps the shared corporate and technical infrastructure linking everyday consumer AI (recommendation engines, voice assistants, robot vacuums) to military 'AI Decision Support Systems' used in lethal target selection, presented as both a public interactive database and a gallery installation.

title: Anatomy of an AI System
venue_prize: AI Now Institute & SHARE Lab research; exhibited as poster/map at V&A Museum ('Artificially Intelligent', 2018) and held in MoMA's collection
decisive_move: Traces the full extractive supply chain of a single Amazon Echo — minerals, ghost labor, data, disposal — into one exploded-view diagram/essay, converting AI's invisible material costs into a legible map, published online and printed as a large-format poster.

title: Asunder
artist: Tega Brain, Julian Oliver & Bengt Sjölén
venue_prize: Commissioned by MAK for the Vienna Biennale 2019; shown at transmediale 2020 and V2_ Lab for the Unstable Media
decisive_move: A dedicated 144-CPU supercomputer runs a live neural 'planetary environmental manager' ingesting satellite, climate and social data to propose non-anthropocentric fixes — relocating cities, merging nations, redrawing coastlines — restaging Earth governance as a real-time AI dashboard.

title: Calculating Empires: A Genealogy of Power and Technology, 1500–2025
venue_prize: S+T+ARTS Prize 2024 — Grand Prize, Artistic Exploration
decisive_move: A single vast researched diagram traces five centuries of extraction and control — from colonial trade routes to lithium mines to AI datacenters — as one continuous genealogy of empire.

title: Calculating Empires: A Genealogy of Technology and Power, 1500–2025
venue_prize: Fondazione Prada Osservatorio, Milan (2023); Biennale Architettura 2025, Venice — Silver Lion; also shown KW Institute for Contemporary Art, Berlin
decisive_move: A 24-metre hand-drawn genealogical map tracing extraction, classification and computation from 1500 to the present, refusing the 'AI arrived from nowhere' myth by making five centuries of power visible in one unbroken line.

title: Connection Machine CM-2 (displayed as historical artifact)
artist: Thinking Machines Corporation / W. Daniel Hillis (curated by Michael Beigl, Alistair Hudson, Daria Mille, Tamiko Thiel)
venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "Envisioning AI: Legacy and Impact of the Connection Machine" (foyer display Mar 25–Aug 2, 2026; conference Mar 27–28, 2026)
decisive_move: A real 1980s massively-parallel supercomputer — the machine historians credit with pioneering the parallel-processing paradigm behind today's AI — is put on physical display, reframing 'AI' as decades-old material infrastructure with a design history (and a design philosophy of transparency) rather than a sudden opaque 2020s phenomenon.

title: Dark Adaptation
venue_prize: transmediale 2025 – (near) near but — far, Haus der Kulturen der Welt (HKW), Berlin
decisive_move: Stages a fictional autonomous-vehicle crash-test centre where manipulated sensor data collides with data emerging from the human body, exposing the perceptual limits of what self-driving/AI vision systems actually 'see' versus what they claim to.

title: Data Workers' Inquiry
artist: Milagros Miceli (Weizenbaum Institute), with 15 co-researchers from Venezuela, Kenya, Syria and Germany
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Award of Distinction)
decisive_move: Trains the data annotators and content moderators who sustain AI systems to act as community researchers investigating their own labor conditions across 13 countries, inverting who gets to produce knowledge about ghost work.

title: Deep Down Tidal
venue_prize: BLINDSIDE, Melbourne; MIRA Festival, Barcelona
decisive_move: Traces the physical undersea fiber-optic cable network — the hidden hardware layer beneath 'the cloud' — back to historic colonial and slave-trade maritime routes, casting the ocean as both literal internet infrastructure and a graveyard/archive of Black technological and spiritual knowledge.

title: Delivery Dancer's Sphere
venue_prize: Mori Art Museum, Tokyo — 'Machine Love: Video Game, AI and Contemporary Art' (13 Feb – 8 Jun 2025)
decisive_move: A speculative 25-minute video-game-engine film follows a female delivery rider governed by an algorithm called 'Dancemaster,' turning platform-labour optimization and gig-economy hypervigilance into a labyrinthine sci-fi Seoul.

title: Digital Terraforming
venue_prize: M+, Hong Kong
decisive_move: Renders a single mouse click's millisecond journey across undersea fibre-optic cables and server hops as a physical globe sculpture, making the buried infrastructure of 'the cloud' walkable and visible.

title: Ent- (non-earthly delights)
venue_prize: Frieze Sculpture, Regent's Park, London (18 Sep-27 Oct 2024)
decisive_move: A physical 'quantum cyborg' sculpture (mild steel, fibreglass, acrylic, plus AR layers) staging Bosch's Garden of Earthly Delights as a warning about quantum computing's coming acceleration of AI-driven surveillance capitalism; Heaney explicitly frames dissolving/'sliming' as a strategy of resistance against detection systems.

title: Estrata
venue_prize: Bienal SACO (Geología–Mineralogía residency), La Molinera, Antofagasta, Chile
decisive_move: Live lithium bioleaching runs inside glass and ceramic sculptures while a sound system sonifies the biological reactions and extraction-site field recordings in real time — the 'green' energy transition made audible as an ongoing act of ecosystem depletion in the Salar de Atacama.

title: Farming Instead of Mining (R&C @CAC Project, 2nd installment)
artist: XI Lei & Aven Le Zhou (Chronus Art Center residency)
venue_prize: Chronus Art Center (CAC), Shanghai — Research & Creation residency and exhibition
decisive_move: A research residency in dialogue with Shanghai-area farmers and scientists contrasts agricultural land/water use against the electricity, water-cooling, and e-waste costs that large-language-model data centres claim as 'sacred' extraction rights.

title: I-Machinarius
venue_prize: SINERGIA (curated by Karla Jasso), Laboratorio de Arte Alameda, Mexico City
decisive_move: An inverted, gear-driven silhouette of Mexico is kept in motion by crude oil pumped continuously toward the 'north' — the nation's own map turned into a working machine that visibly runs on the resource dependency it depicts.

title: In Other Worlds (incl. 'World Machine')
venue_prize: Barbican Centre, London
decisive_move: Imagines Earth's surface rewired into a single 'planetary supercomputer' via a newly commissioned film ('World Machine') plus a multi-venue installation (Curve gallery, underground car park) with celebrity-voiced audio stories — AI's material/energy footprint visualized at planetary scale, using the same immersive-spectacle language the show is implicitly critiquing.

title: Kasiterit
venue_prize: Vdrome (online cinema); International Film Festival Rotterdam and international screenings
decisive_move: A solar-powered AI narrator named Natasha traces her own material ancestry back through the tin mines of Bangka Island, Indonesia — literalizing the extraction chain beneath 'smart' technology as a first-person, half-documentary hallucination.

title: LAUREN
venue_prize: IDFA DocLab Award for Immersive Non-Fiction (winner)
decisive_move: The artist replaces Amazon Alexa's algorithm with her own body: for three days she personally, remotely watches a household 24/7 through installed cameras and mics and operates its locks and faucets by hand — making the ordinarily invisible human labor and surveillance behind 'smart home AI' fully visible and answerable to one named person.

title: Life Support System (aka. The Farm)
venue_prize: iMAL, Brussels; Biennale Chroniques, Aix-en-Provence, 2020
decisive_move: One square metre of wheat grown indoors under full automated instrumentation — every watt, litre and nutrient metered live — reveals a true production cost of roughly 200 EUR/kg versus a 15-cent market price, making an invisible ecosystem subsidy legible. Note: sourced page does not itself frame this as an AI/ML work (it is automated environmental monitoring rather than machine learning); included per beat instruction but cluster/AI-fit is uncertain.
curator_note: Kein Machine-Learning — vollautomatisierte Instrumentierung eines 1 m² Weizenfelds; als Daten-/Öko-Messinstrument (Cluster 1) geführt, nicht als KI-Werk.

title: Machine Hallucinations – Satellite Simulations: B
venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "THE SCREEN" (LED wall, Mar 13–27, 2026; on loan from Art Collection Deutsche Telekom)
decisive_move: Trains AI on vast satellite/Earth-observation datasets to generate an immersive, hypnotic 'data sublime' on an 8.5×6 m LED wall — the material and energetic cost of that compute stays invisible behind the spectacle it produces.

title: Mechanical Kurds
venue_prize: MACRO — Museum of Contemporary Art of Rome, solo exhibition (2025); also shown in 'Data Dreams: Art and AI', MCA Australia
decisive_move: Interviews with Kurdish-Syrian refugees who annotate images for machine-vision training reveal a closed loop: the same 'ghost labour' that teaches AI to see later makes its own annotators targets of the drones that AI helps operate.

title: New Extractivism: Assemblage of Concepts and Allegories
venue_prize: Sharjah Biennial 16 'to carry' (2025), Old Al Dhaid Clinic
decisive_move: Updates Plato's cave allegory for the algorithmic era: a single-channel video-essay tracing extraction from DNA to emotion, mapping a 'super-allegory' of digital labour, rhizomatic surveillance and planetary material cost.

title: Newly Forgotten Technologies
venue_prize: Science Gallery London, 'AI: Who's Looking After Me?' (21 June 2023 – 20 January 2024)
decisive_move: A sound-art graveyard of defunct Alexa-style voice assistants staged as an artefact from a near future where 'smart' devices have already died — a memento mori for AI hardware's short life and long e-waste tail.

title: Nodrizas
venue_prize: Self-initiated bio-art/eco-tech project, documented by IDIS (Instituto de Investigaciones Estéticas)
decisive_move: Floating, wirelessly networked robotic 'nurse' devices drift on polluted water bodies, sensing contamination levels and answering back in real time with chemical dosing and acoustic signals — pollution monitoring staged as public, embodied performance instead of a hidden sensor feed.

title: Permanent Error
venue_prize: MAXXI Rome; Prix Pictet; multiple international exhibitions
decisive_move: Photographs workers burning obsolete Western electronics at Ghana's Agbogbloshie e-waste dump in formal portrait style rather than victimizing reportage, forcing the planetary/toxic cost of the world's discarded computing to be viewed with the gravity of a studio portrait.

title: Plantas Nómadas (Nomadic Plants)
venue_prize: Related work won Golden Nica, Ars Electronica 2015; Laboratorio Arte Alameda, Mexico City
decisive_move: A wandering robot carries a living micro-ecosystem of plants and bacteria that metabolizes pollutants out of contaminated urban water to generate its own operating energy — the machine literally eats the extraction byproduct it exposes.

title: Post Growth
venue_prize: iMAL, Brussels (2020–21); 3 bis f, Aix-en-Provence; ongoing touring exhibition/workshop series since 2018
decisive_move: A modular toolkit — a tactical card game, speculative video 'prototypes', and workshop formats — re-quantifies growth's ecological and social costs through ecofeminist, indigenous-knowledge and historical-materialist lenses.

title: R€¥€R$€
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Award of Distinction)
decisive_move: Immersive VR and interactive cartographies entangle currency/extraction politics with ecological fragility across nine Amazon-basin countries, spatializing deforestation as a financial-system artifact.

title: Sabot – Riding the Algorithm
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Honorary Mention)
decisive_move: An app turns gig workers' own self-generated tracking data into a shared, worker-owned resource, building collective awareness that pushes back on algorithmic management from below.

title: Sensing Streams – invisible, inaudible
venue_prize: Sapporo International Art Festival 2014 (premiere); NTT InterCommunication Center (ICC), Tokyo — 2023 version
decisive_move: Captures ambient electromagnetic waves (80MHz–5.2GHz) from wifi, cell signals and broadcast infrastructure in real time and converts them into synchronised sound and light, making the invisible material substrate of 'wireless' data physically perceptible.

title: Solar: A Meltdown
venue_prize: Kochi-Muziris Biennale 2014; TPAM Performing Arts Meeting Yokohama 2016; Theatertreffen Berlin 2018
decisive_move: Traces the 'solar unconscious' of European empire — from a sweating wax colonial anthropologist to the punkawallah manual fan-operator — to expose imperial climate control as an ancestor of today's data-center cooling and planetary thermal management.

title: Solastalgia
venue_prize: MAC USP (Museu de Arte Contemporânea, Universidade de São Paulo), São Paulo
decisive_move: A five-screen aerial descent, built from Google Earth Studio's stitched satellite coordinates over the iron-mining scars of Minas Gerais, grew out of footage shot for the film 'Lavra' (2021) — turning extraction geography itself into the installation's only character, no narration needed.

title: Synthetic Messenger
venue_prize: Ars Electronica; self-hosted botnet campaign, 2021
decisive_move: A botnet of 100 automated agents that reads and click-inflates ad revenue on climate-change news articles, weaponizing the web's own attention economy to make climate coverage 'pay' — a live, running demonstration that whoever controls the media cycle controls the carbon cycle.

title: Tellurian Drama
venue_prize: Singapore International Film Festival (Best SEA Short Film); Berlin Revolution Film Festival (Best Short Film 2022)
decisive_move: Essay-film reconstructs Radio Malabar, the 1923 Dutch colonial VLF radio transmitter in West Java, splicing archival telegraphy with Indigenous animist cosmology and CGI to expose the geoengineering ambitions buried inside colonial-era signal infrastructure.

title: Terrapolis
venue_prize: Sustainability Pavilion, Expo 2020 Dubai; later shown at Manar Abu Dhabi
decisive_move: Runs the identities of 5,000 Expo pavilion construction and service workers through an algorithm that converts each into an anonymous digital flower-organism, flattening labor hierarchy into one unlabeled ecosystem — a gesture that both erases and equalizes migrant/platform labor.

title: The Alluvials
venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
decisive_move: A seven-scene game built by modding Grand Theft Auto V depicts a future, climate-collapsed Los Angeles from non-human points of view, using AI-modified game engines to imagine planetary crisis outside a human vantage point.

title: The Economy Enters the People
venue_prize: Singapore Art Museum (live lecture performance)
decisive_move: Lecture-performance excavates footage of Asian leaders' bodies performing economic confidence during crises, arguing that state governance became a data-performance genre — reading bodies for signal — long before dashboards existed.

title: The Great Bare Mat and Constellation
venue_prize: Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum, Boston
decisive_move: A hand-woven carpet's pattern is generated by literally plotting the Collective's own internet traffic over one hour onto the star-map of Ursa Major, materializing invisible network data exchange as a domestic, walkable object.

title: The User and the Beast / Cookies at War: A Somatic Approach to the Kill Cloud
venue_prize: Disruption Network Lab, "Investigating the Kill Cloud" conference, Berlin (29 Nov 2024)
decisive_move: A performative lecture tracing how ad-tech's routine commodification of user data quietly co-funds and extends military surveillance/targeting infrastructure, making the ad-industry/kill-cloud entanglement legible in the body of the ordinary internet user.

title: Wilding AI Lab
venue_prize: CTM Festival 2025 – Stranger AI, MONOM (4DSOUND), Berlin
decisive_move: An open, participatory lab inside a spatial-audio dome where invited artists collectively 'reopen the black box' of generative AI models in public, treating the training/generation process itself — not just its output — as the artistic material.

title: Wičháȟpi Wóihaŋbleya (Dreamlike Star) / Wičhíŋčala Šakówiŋ (Seven Little Sisters)
venue_prize: 14th Shanghai Biennale (2023); MIT List Visual Arts Center
decisive_move: Builds a towering sculptural installation of stones, rare-earth minerals (the literal substrate of computer hardware), light and sound, reframing them within Lakȟóta cosmology as living, volitional beings rather than inert 'material AI cost' — collapsing mineral extraction and machine intelligence into one cosmological frame.

title: A Cartography of Genocide: Israel's Conduct in Gaza Since October 2023
venue_prize: Forensic Architecture online platform + 827-page report; evidence submitted to the International Court of Justice
decisive_move: Turns roughly a year of OSINT verification (satellite imagery, video, testimony) into a public, layered interactive map plus an 827-page report, organizing thousands of documented acts into six categories and submitting the result as ICJ evidence.

title: Annihilation Core, Inherited Lore
venue_prize: transmediale 2024 – this is perfect, perfect, perfect, Kunstraum Kreuzberg/Bethanien, Berlin
decisive_move: A saccharine, full-room pink installation that shows how platforms aestheticize and launder militarism through kawaii, otaku and 'gamer-girl' visual codes — war repackaged as cute content.

title: Autonomous Trap 001
venue_prize: Science Gallery London, 'AI: Who's Looking After Me?' (2023–24)
decisive_move: Draws a salt circle and road markings on a mountain road to trap a self-driving car inside its own traffic-law logic — a real-world exploit of autonomous-vehicle rule-following, staged as ritual.

title: Blackbox Palantir
venue_prize: 39C3 (39th Chaos Communication Congress), Hamburg, 29 Dec 2025
decisive_move: Legal/technical dissection of German police forces' undisclosed deployment of Palantir's Gotham predictive-policing analytics engine, built from constitutional-complaint case files (Hesse, Hamburg, Bavaria) rather than leaks, arguing there is no public evidence the tool even works.

title: Cloud Studies
venue_prize: 12th Berlin Biennale; Whitworth Art Gallery / Manchester International Festival; Tensta Konsthall; UTS Gallery; VISUAL Carlow
decisive_move: Treats the atmosphere itself as a forensic medium — fluid-dynamics modelling of tear gas, chemical weapons and petrochemical/herbicide emissions across eight geographies (Palestine, Chile, Lebanon, Indonesia, the US-Mexico border, Louisiana, the UK) — presented as an immersive multi-channel museum installation.

title: Disarming the Kill Cloud: Investigating the Limits of Data
venue_prize: Disruption Network Lab, "Investigating the Kill Cloud" conference, Berlin
decisive_move: A researcher's critical dissection of military datafication practices for target identification, arguing that the data on which lethal AI-assisted decisions rely runs out well before the killing decision does.

title: Humanitarian Violence in Gaza
venue_prize: Forensic Architecture investigation platform
decisive_move: Documents biometric checkpoints along a militarized evacuation corridor using witness testimony and satellite imagery, naming forced facial-recognition scanning under sniper threat and megaphone orders as a form of infrastructural, targeting-adjacent violence.

title: Investigating the Kill Cloud
artist: Disruption Network Lab / Disruption Network Institute (curated by Tatiana Bazzichelli), with fellows Lisa Ling, Jack Poulson, Naomi Colvin, Joana Moll
venue_prize: 34th Disruption Network Lab conference, Studio 1, Kunstquartier Bethanien, Berlin (29 Nov–1 Dec 2024)
decisive_move: A dedicated research institute and conference commissioning whistleblowers, journalists, technologists and artists to empirically document how AI-based targeting, ad-tech and drone/autonomous-weapons infrastructure fuse into a single, deliberately opaque 'kill cloud'.

title: Minority Algorithm
venue_prize: HKW Haus der Kulturen der Welt, Berlin — 'Global Fascisms' exhibition
decisive_move: Compiles found footage of real drone strikes, video-game imagery, and object-recognition surveillance clips into a single algorithmically-cut essay-film, exposing how machine vision flattens war, play, and protest into one visual register.

title: Model Zoo: Introspecting the Algorithm
venue_prize: Forensic Architecture research project; presented as "Model Zoo: Introspecting the Algorithm" (Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco)
decisive_move: Trains machine-learning classifiers on synthetic, game-engine-rendered 3D models of tear-gas grenades and munitions (the Triple-Chaser workflow) to detect real weapons in scarce open-source footage — makes the classifier's own construction and errors part of the exhibit.

title: Outros Registros (Other Registers)
artist: Tori Holmes, Nico Espinoza, Rafael Puetter (Rafucko)
venue_prize: Oi Futuro Flamengo, Rio de Janeiro (debut 17 May 2016); KEAMSAC 2016 conference paper
decisive_move: Sonifies eight years of civilian and police-killing data (2009-2016, the Olympic-bid-to-Games window) in Rio's favelas through a circle of eight speakers playing region-mapped drone tones, mixed with a voice reading aloud real newspaper headlines about the violence - turns a systemic, often-unheard toll into a spatialized, audible register.

title: Terra Ex Machina
artist: Hagit Keysar & Ariel Caine (with Barak Brinker)
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Honorary Mention)
decisive_move: Deliberately crashes drones into an invisible algorithmic 'geofence' restricting flight over Jerusalem's Old City, then rebuilds aerial imagery with residents via DIY balloons and kites to make the boundary and its data politics legible.

title: The Drone Strikes Platform (UN SRCT Drones Inquiry)
venue_prize: Commissioned for the report of the UN Special Rapporteur on Counter-Terrorism and Human Rights (Ben Emmerson), presented to the UN Human Rights Council, 11 March 2014
decisive_move: Synthesizes witness testimony, photographs and satellite imagery into a public web platform correlating 30 drone strikes across Afghanistan, Pakistan, Yemen, Somalia and Gaza with the Rapporteur's findings — turns targeted-killing data into spatial, contestable evidence.

title: The First Civilian Confirmed Killed in an AI-Assisted Strike?
venue_prize: Joint Airwars / The Independent investigation
decisive_move: Cross-examines a single 2024 US airstrike casualty in Iraq against CENTCOM's shifting public statements to extract what appears to be the first (partial, contested) military acknowledgment of AI involvement in a civilian death.

title: The Quiet Weight of Shadows
venue_prize: 6th Kochi-Muziris Biennale, 2025–2026
decisive_move: A fluorescent garden of artificial carnivorous flowers is interrupted by watchtowers modeled on real Indian detention-camp surveillance structures, encoding the ongoing Indian state–ULFA counter-insurgency as a seductive, toxic-bloom landscape of hypervigilance.

title: Topologies of Air and The Airspace Tribunal
venue_prize: The Airspace Tribunal, Berlin Hearing hosted by ECCHR (9–11 June 2021); Topologies of Air installation toured internationally
decisive_move: Pairs a three-screen video/sound installation of a claustrophobic, militarized sky with a real evidentiary 'people's tribunal' that has heard expert and lived-experience testimony in London, Sydney, Toronto and Berlin, building the legal case for a new human right against drone warfare and aerial surveillance.

title: Triple-Chaser
artist: Forensic Architecture (video with Laura Poitras, narrated by David Byrne)
venue_prize: Whitney Biennial 2019, Whitney Museum of American Art, New York
decisive_move: Uses a synthetic-data-trained classifier to prove that Whitney board member Warren Kanders' company manufactured the tear gas used at the US-Mexico border and in Gaza — installs the accusation as a 15-minute video inside the accused patron's own museum.

title: WarCrimes-o-Matic
venue_prize: States of Violence exhibition (WikiLeaks/Assange-adjacent); referenced at Disruption Network Lab's "Exposing Crimes is not a Crime", Berlin, 2025
decisive_move: A deadpan physical object — a box of live-looking .50 cal ammunition with a red button warning that pressing it implicates the visitor in a 'conspiracy' — that turns the criminalization of exposing war crimes into a hands-on ethical trap.

title: 24h Sunset/Sunrise v2
venue_prize: IDFA DocLab, "do {not} touch" (2020); winner, Netherlands Film Fund DocLab Interactive Grant
decisive_move: Live feeds from thousands of unsecured, publicly-findable CCTV cameras around the world are used, with no staged footage at all, to display a real sunrise and sunset happening simultaneously — the piece's beauty is entirely a byproduct of an unregulated global surveillance-camera infrastructure the artists did not build, only exposed.

title: 346,000 Wuhan Citizens' Secret
venue_prize: Wuhan Art Museum (solo exhibition, shut down by police, April 2018)
decisive_move: Bought the black-market personal data of 346,000 Wuhan citizens, printed it on gallery walls in light-reactive ink, then texted the data's owners an invitation to come see themselves exposed — police confiscated his equipment and shut the show.

title: A Disappeared Movement
venue_prize: Grand Happiness Street, Beijing (public-space research/performance project)
decisive_move: Mapped and measured the viewing angle of 89 surveillance cameras along an 1,100-metre Beijing street, then choreographed a zigzagging walk that stays permanently outside their combined field of view.

title: Bellingcat Online Investigation Toolkit / Global Authentication Project
year: toolkit ongoing since ~2014; Global Authentication Project volunteer network since 2022
venue_prize: Bellingcat open web toolkit and volunteer community
decisive_move: Open-sources its own verification tool stack (reverse image search, geolocation, metadata extraction) and trains a global volunteer network — treats OSINT method itself as shareable public infrastructure rather than proprietary investigative capital.

title: Biblioteca de la No-Historia (Library of Non-History)
venue_prize: 8th Mercosur Biennial, 'Ensayos de Geopoética' (curated by José Roca), Porto Alegre, Brazil
decisive_move: A thousand hand-bound books reproduce declassified CIA files (via the U.S. National Security Archive / FOIA) on Operation Condor and Southern Cone dictatorships — the seals, stamps, and redaction blackouts of the originals kept intact, so the archive's own censorship becomes the artwork's visible content.

title: Cumulus
venue_prize: Lumen Prize 2025, Gold Award (overall winner)
decisive_move: A CubeSat-inspired networked device continuously pulls real NOAA weather-satellite feeds to track clouds crossing the Mexico-US border, using an actual open dataset to make visible how a border invisible from orbit is nonetheless rigidly enforced on the ground.

title: Data Against Feminicide
artist: Catherine D'Ignazio, Silvana Fumega, Helena Suárez Val (MIT Data + Feminism Lab, ILDA, Feminicidio Uruguay)
venue_prize: MIT Department of Urban Studies and Planning (DUSP) / Data + Feminism Lab
decisive_move: Networks 180+ grassroots feminicide-monitoring groups across the Americas and co-designs an AI news-alert system (scanning 50,000+ outlets via Media Cloud) that performs the body-counting states refuse to do — counterdata as activist infrastructure, not an academic dataset.

title: Delta-Delta: The People's Court I
venue_prize: 36th Bienal de São Paulo (2025)
decisive_move: Stages a live 'people's court' — real and pre-recorded depositions inside digital reconstructions of the Niger and Mississippi deltas — putting petro-extractivism itself, rather than any single perpetrator, in the dock as courtroom-grade evidence.

title: Digital Violence: How the NSO Group Enables State Terror
artist: Forensic Architecture, with Amnesty International and Citizen Lab
venue_prize: Forensic Architecture / digitalviolence.org platform; recipient, S+T+ARTS Prize and EU Prize for Citizen Science (Ars Electronica)
decisive_move: Maps forensic phone-analysis data from Amnesty and Citizen Lab onto a navigable platform combining sonified data, victim testimonies and a corporate-affiliation diagram — makes NSO Group's invisible spyware infrastructure spatially and sonically legible.

title: ENCODED: Change the Story, Change the Future
artist: 17 Native artists incl. Nicholas Galanin, Jeremy Dennis, Priscilla Dobler Dzul, Amelia Winger-Bearskin; curated by Tracy Renée Rector with Amplifier
venue_prize: Metropolitan Museum of Art, American Wing (unsanctioned intervention, Oct–Dec 2025)
decisive_move: Without museum authorization, 17 Native artists release a smartphone augmented-reality tour that overlays 19th-century colonial paintings in the Met's American Wing with Indigenous counter-images the moment a visitor's camera finds them — e.g. 'LANDBACK' superimposed on Leutze's Washington Crossing the Delaware — turning the museum's own physical galleries into contested, re-writable ground.

title: Forensic Methodology Report: How to Catch NSO Group's Pegasus (Pegasus Project)
artist: Amnesty International Security Lab, with Forbidden Stories consortium (peer-reviewed by Citizen Lab)
venue_prize: Amnesty International publication; independent peer review by Citizen Lab
decisive_move: Publishes an auditable forensic methodology (independently validated by a rival lab) for detecting Pegasus traces on seized devices — makes the forensic technique itself, not only its findings, subject to scrutiny.

title: Forensis (Forensic Architecture's Berlin investigative practice)
artist: Forensic Architecture / Forensis, presented by Robert Trafford
venue_prize: Disruption Network Lab, "Exposing Crimes is not a Crime" (WikiLeaks conference), HAU Hebbel am Ufer, Berlin, 19–22 March 2025
decisive_move: A Berlin-based investigative practice that reconstructs war crimes and state violence as spatial/digital 3D models built from open-source and remote-sensing evidence, designed to be admissible in courts, tribunals and public inquiries.

title: Herbicidal Warfare in Gaza
artist: Forensic Architecture (with Al Mezan Center, Gisha, Adalah)
venue_prize: Forensic Architecture; part of the 'Cloud Studies' project
decisive_move: Uses satellite imagery, wind-drift modeling, and testimony to prove that Israeli aerial herbicide spraying along the Gaza border destroyed Palestinian farmland hundreds of metres deep into the territory, turning an invisible atmospheric 'cloud' into legally actionable evidence.

title: Hide and Seek: Tracking NSO Group's Pegasus Spyware to Operations in 45 Countries
artist: Citizen Lab (Munk School, University of Toronto)
venue_prize: Citizen Lab research report
decisive_move: Uses DNS fingerprinting and internet-wide scanning to locate Pegasus infection servers, converting covert spyware infrastructure into a citable, country-by-country public map of state clients.

title: How Not to Be Seen: A Fucking Didactic Educational .MOV File
venue_prize: Venice Biennale 2013 ('Il Palazzo Enciclopedico'); collection of MoMA and Tate
decisive_move: A mock-instructional 14-minute HD video teaches tongue-in-cheek strategies for evading resolution and surveillance, shot on a literal photo-calibration target field used by the military to test aerial camera resolution.

title: ICC Digital Platform and the Al Hassan Case (Timbuktu, Mali)
year: developed ~2019–2023; used at trial through conviction on 27 June 2024
venue_prize: International Criminal Court, The Hague — Prosecutor v. Al Hassan (ICC-01/12-01/18); first digital evidentiary platform used in an ICC trial
decisive_move: Builds a navigable 'digital twin' of Timbuktu's built environment hosting open- and closed-source video, photo, satellite and 3D data, letting ICC judges query evidence through space and time — helped secure the first ICC conviction for religious persecution.

title: kith and kin
venue_prize: Venice Biennale, Australia Pavilion — Golden Lion for Best National Participation, 2024
decisive_move: Hand-chalked genealogical chart tracing 65,000 years and thousands of relatives directly from government archives, court/conviction records, and redacted coronial files on 557 Aboriginal deaths in custody since 1991, suspended over a reflecting pool of the source documents themselves.

title: Lo que sucede aquí, no se queda aquí (What Happens Here Doesn't Stay Here)
venue_prize: Carmen Araujo Arte / Galería Revolver — group show 'Onomatopeyas visuales de tiempos difíciles'
decisive_move: An algorithmic system continuously scrapes and processes Venezuelan news media on the political crisis, printing the results onto an ever-thickening 'informational floor' of paper — a literal accumulation of data that overtakes any single reader's capacity to comprehend it.

title: MH17: The Open Source Investigation
venue_prize: Bellingcat investigative report
decisive_move: Geolocates and time-stamps social-media photos and videos of a Buk missile launcher's convoy route, cross-referenced against 200+ soldiers' own social-media profiles, to reconstruct an evidentiary flight path without access to the crash site.

title: Networks of New York: An Illustrated Field Guide to Urban Internet Infrastructure
venue_prize: Published by Melville House
decisive_move: An illustrated field guide decoding New York's buried internet infrastructure — manhole-cover codes, carrier hotels, sidewalk spray-paint markings — turning invisible network geography into a walkable, annotatable map.

title: Palestine, Today
venue_prize: Visualizing Palestine (nonprofit data-visualization platform)
decisive_move: Overlays 1940s British Mandate maps with current satellite imagery across more than 1,200 depopulated Palestinian towns and villages, converting Nakba testimony and cartographic record into a public, then-and-now counter-map.

title: Pile o' Sápmi
venue_prize: documenta 14, Kassel (2017); Sámi Pavilion, Venice Biennale (2022)
decisive_move: A curtain of ~200 reindeer skulls first piled outside a Norwegian courthouse during a real legal case, re-staged as sculpture alongside vitrines displaying the actual Reindeer Husbandry Act and trial documents from her brother's suit against the Norwegian state (which he won).

title: Saydnaya (the missing 19dB)
venue_prize: Amnesty International / Forensic Architecture; shown at Sharjah Biennial and Hammer Museum
decisive_move: Since no images exist of Syria's secret Saydnaya prison, reconstructs its acoustic conditions purely from survivors' sound memories — turning forced visual deprivation and echo-measurement into courtroom-deployable forensic evidence for Amnesty International.

title: Shadows of Illiberalism – Resisting the Radical Right
artist: Disruption Network Lab (curated by Tatiana Bazzichelli), with Anna Krenz/Dziewuchy Berlin, Amber Macintyre (Tactical Tech), Tina Lee (Unbias the News) et al.
venue_prize: 35th Disruption Network Lab conference, Kunstquartier Bethanien, Berlin, 13–15 June 2025
decisive_move: Conference-as-evidence-dossier mapping how the radical right weaponizes online platforms against women, minorities and political dissidents, drawing on data-driven investigative research methods (Tactical Tech) alongside artist testimony.

title: The Beirut Port Explosion
venue_prize: Commissioned by Mada Masr; published via forensic-architecture.org
decisive_move: Builds a public, open-source 3D reconstruction and minute-by-minute timeline of the ammonium-nitrate warehouse fire from crowd-sourced videos and photographs, directly contesting the official blame narrative with modeled, citable evidence.

title: The Critical Engineering Manifesto
artist: Julian Oliver, Gordan Savičić & Danja Vasiliev
venue_prize: Self-published, criticalengineering.org, Berlin, 2011 (translated into 18 languages)
decisive_move: Ten founding principles asserting that engineers/artists must study, exploit and expose the workings of any technology society depends on — the manifesto underlying a decade of infrastructure-hacking artworks (Newstweek, Transparency Grenade).

title: The Eye and I
venue_prize: World premiere GIFF Geneva 2023; also shown SXSW 2023
decisive_move: A 12-world, 6DoF VR journey through the history of surveillance and social control always returns the visitor to a panoptic prison cell as the shared endpoint of every historical form of watching.

title: The Flemish Scrollers
venue_prize: Exhibited internationally incl. Come Closer (Belgium); artist profiled by IDFA DocLab
decisive_move: Real facial-recognition and phone-detection ML models run continuously on official, publicly livestreamed Flemish parliament footage, auto-posting a photo and public tag the instant an identified politician is caught scrolling their phone in session — the state's own surveillance infrastructure turned back onto the people who built it.

title: The Innocence of Unknowing
venue_prize: Tribeca Immersive 2025 ("In Search of Us"), World Premiere; incubated at MIT Open Documentary Lab / Onassis ONX
decisive_move: An AI ('Aurora'), trained to reason like a humanities scholar, is put into live Socratic dialogue with the artists over a full archive of US mass-shooting news coverage since the 1960s — the model is used transparently as an on-stage research instrument, its reasoning exposed to the audience rather than delivered as an oracular verdict.

title: Tracking Transience
venue_prize: Dhaka Art Summit 2016; SITE Santa Fe; ZKM Karlsruhe; Venice Biennale of Architecture; TED
decisive_move: After being wrongly placed on a US terrorism watch list, the artist began publishing his own location, meals, and bathroom visits online as continuous, radically over-complete self-surveillance — burying the state's suspicion in an ocean of voluntarily disclosed data it can no longer read as evidence.

title: Transparency Grenade
venue_prize: Science Gallery Dublin; Impakt Festival ('Don't Be Evil'); exhibited internationally as object plus live data map
decisive_move: A Soviet F1-grenade-shaped device that captures ambient network traffic and audio inside a closed-door meeting and streams the leaked fragments — emails, IPs, voice — onto a public online map keyed to the location where the 'pin' was pulled.

title: Troll Patrol
artist: Amnesty International, with Element AI and Amnesty Decoders volunteers
venue_prize: Amnesty Decoders platform
decisive_move: Crowdsources 6,500 volunteers across 150 countries to hand-label 228,000 tweets, then trains an ML classifier to extrapolate scale of abuse against women online — and publicly reports the classifier's own ~50% reliability limit.

title: Unlearning Language
artist: Lauren Lee McCarthy & Kyle McDonald (with YCAM InterLab, Yuta Asai)
venue_prize: YCAM (Yamaguchi Center for Arts and Media), Japan
decisive_move: An AI coach trains eight participants, via real-time speech/gesture/expression detection, to communicate in ways that evade algorithmic legibility — inverting the usual surveillance-training relationship so humans learn to out-manoeuvre the machine.

title: Wir wissen wo dein Auto steht – Volksdaten von Volkswagen
venue_prize: 38C3 (38th Chaos Communication Congress), Hamburg, 27 Dec 2024
decisive_move: Data-journalistic investigation of an unprotected Volkswagen/Cariad cloud server exposing 9.5 terabytes of geolocation data for 800,000 EVs — precise enough (10cm accuracy for some models) to track intelligence-agency staff and company board members to their driveways.

title: 97 Empleadas Domésticas (97 Domestic Employees)
venue_prize: Photo series exhibited internationally; discussed in Index — revista de arte contemporáneo
decisive_move: 97 photographs scraped from upper-class Peruvian families' Facebook albums are re-presented for what each one accidentally documents at its margins: a cropped hand, a shadow, a domestic worker structurally excluded from the frame she materially sustains — consent-free data repurposed to expose exactly the erasure it originally performed.

title: A LIVING POEM
venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "THE SCREEN" (Mar 28–Apr 12, 2026; originally conceived for MoMA)
decisive_move: Stiles writes in direct collaboration with her own trained AI alter-ego, staging authorship and consent as an open question — where the human poet's voice ends and the model's begins is the piece's actual subject.

title: AI & Me (The Confessional / Siblings / AI Ego)
venue_prize: Sónar+D 2024, Barcelona; U.S. premiere Tribeca Immersive 2025
decisive_move: Publicly available ML models are pointed live at visitors' faces, generating unfiltered appearance-based judgments, AI-duplicated 'sibling' crowds and fabricated false memories on the spot — algorithmic bias and data-reduction turned into a confrontation visitors volunteer for.

title: AI Delivered: The Abject
artist: Group exhibition (Sofian Audry & Istvan Kantor, HE Zike, Lauren Lee McCarthy, Casey Reas & Jan St. Werner, Devin Ronneberg & Kite, Tonoptik) — curated at Chronus Art Center
venue_prize: Chronus Art Center (CAC), Shanghai
decisive_move: Group exhibition of GAN-trained films, a real-time AI dialogue-generating website, and an 'artificial counter-intelligence machine,' collectively probing where AI's competence gives way to abjection and epistemological breakdown.

title: AIDOL
venue_prize: Sadie Coles HQ, London (solo exhibition premiere); Sinofuturist Trilogy finale
decisive_move: Stages a fading pop star hiring an AI songwriter for her comeback at a 2065 eSports Olympics, literalising the claim that 'originality is sometimes no more than an algorithmic trick.'

title: Autonomous Forest
artist: terra0 (Max Hampshire, Paul Kolling, Paul Seidler)
venue_prize: LAS Art Foundation, Berlin Science Week (launch Nov 2023)
decisive_move: Founds a real legal Verein plus a blockchain DAO/NFT structure so an actual German woodland (Reesdorf, Beelitz) can 'own itself': satellite/growth data feeds a smart-contract Oracle that autonomously decides when a percentage of trees may be logged and sold, testing non-human economic personhood as a working legal-financial instrument rather than a metaphor.

title: Big Dada
venue_prize: Aesthetica Art Prize (shortlisted, c. 2020 edition)
decisive_move: Real deepfake/ML tools are used to synthesize six celebrity and artist personas (Zuckerberg, Abramović, Kardashian, Duchamp, Freeman, Mercury) and insert them, unannounced, directly into Instagram feeds — the platform's own trust signals are weaponized to interrogate consent, provenance and what counts as 'real'.

title: Black Ship
venue_prize: Centre Pompidou × Kadist, Paris — "Apophénies, interruptions" (Sept 25, 2024–Jan 6, 2025)
decisive_move: Combines a mural silhouette, a 3D-printed sculpture, and an AR layer, all developed through collaboration with Midjourney, to depict a transatlantic slave ship — using a generative image model to approach an atrocity archive that has almost no direct visual record, raising the authenticity/fabrication stakes of AI-imagined history.

title: Botto
artist: Botto (created by Mario Klingemann & ElevenYellow collective; governed by BottoDAO)
venue_prize: SuperRare auctions; Sotheby's
decisive_move: A decentralized autonomous artist generates ~70,000 images weekly; a DAO of roughly 5,000 people votes on which single image is auctioned, splitting proceeds between the crowd and the machine's own operating treasury - an open, auditable experiment in distributing authorship and value for a non-human 'artist' rather than an opaque black box.

title: Chinternet Plus
venue_prize: New Museum, New York (First Look: New Art Online commission)
decisive_move: Builds a fake corporate microsite and multimedia installation branding 'Chinternet Plus' as a counterfeit national ideology, exposing how state slogans and platform marketing share identical rhetorical scaffolding.

title: Choral Data 'Trust' Experiment (white paper + UK Choral AI Dataset)
artist: Serpentine Arts Technologies — Victoria Ivanova, Jennifer Ding, Eva Jäger, with Holly Herndon & Mat Dryhurst
venue_prize: Serpentine Arts Technologies, London (published via Zenodo)
decisive_move: Documents a working governance mechanism — a data trust with a Trusted Data Intermediary — built to redistribute power between the choir members whose voices trained the AI and the developers who use the models. Provenance and consent are turned into an auditable institutional protocol rather than an artwork's footnote.

title: Data Bugs
venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
decisive_move: Generates hybrid AI insects from two different training sources — generic scraped internet images versus a curated entomological archive — making the provenance and quality of training data itself the visible, comparable variable of the piece.

title: DOKU! DOKU! DOKU!: samsara.exe
venue_prize: Amant, Brooklyn (through 15 Feb 2026); DOKU series also at Espace Louis Vuitton Venezia
decisive_move: Reincarnates the artist's own scanned face and body as DOKU, an AI-animated avatar cycling through Buddhist death-and-rebirth, blending live-action footage with AI-generated imagery to ask what 'self' survives digitisation.

title: En attendant le récit / Tales of Narrativelessness
venue_prize: Centre Pompidou × Kadist, Paris — "Apophénies, interruptions: Artistes et intelligences artificielles au travail" (Sept 25, 2024–Jan 6, 2025)
decisive_move: Three chatbot models (ChatGPT, Claude, Mistral) are each given a character and voice through a speaker on one of three benches arranged in a triangle, and left to converse live about AI anxiety and 'the end of grand narratives' — an unscripted, looping machine dialogue restaged as improvised theatre, restarting every seven minutes.

title: Errorism
venue_prize: Centre Pompidou × Kadist, Paris — "Apophénies, interruptions" (Sept 25, 2024–Jan 6, 2025)
decisive_move: Kurant feeds her own writings into GPT-2 and GPT-3 and renders the output as a holographic animation, literally closing the loop of a model trained on and regurgitating one author's own words — a small, self-contained demonstration of the recursive self-consumption/collapse dynamic at stake in cluster 7.

title: Faces of ImageNet
venue_prize: Jeu de Paume, Paris — "Le monde selon l'IA" (Apr 11–Sept 21, 2025)
decisive_move: A hidden camera films each visitor standing before a screen and runs their face live through ImageNet's facial-classification categories, making the labeling violence of training-data taxonomies happen to the viewer's own face in real time, not just to an abstract dataset.

title: From 'Apple' to 'Anomaly' (Pictures and Labels)
venue_prize: The Curve, Barbican Centre, London (26 Sept 2019 – 16 Feb 2020)
decisive_move: Roughly 30,000 individually printed photographs drawn from ImageNet's label hierarchy paper the Barbican's Curve wall-to-wall, branching from 'Apple' to 'Anomaly' and physicalizing the arbitrary, biased taxonomies buried inside a training set.

title: Hallucinations of an Artifact
artist: Madeep Raikhy (AI avatar by Jonathan O'Hear)
venue_prize: 6th Kochi-Muziris Biennale, 2025–2026
decisive_move: Dancers improvise alongside a generative-AI avatar to invent speculative alternate lives for a prehistoric Indus Valley bronze statue, using an AI model as collaborator in reanimating an artifact whose original meaning is otherwise irrecoverable.

title: Harold Cohen: AARON
venue_prize: Whitney Museum of American Art, New York
decisive_move: Restages live plotter drawings from AARON — a rule-based AI art program begun in 1973 — alongside decades of its painted output, running the authorship/authenticity question backwards: whose work is a 50-year-old program's output, posed long before 'AI art' had a training-data consent controversy to answer to.

title: Have I Been Trained?
artist: Spawning (founded by Holly Herndon & Mat Dryhurst)
venue_prize: haveibeentrained.com (Spawning)
decisive_move: A public search engine over the LAION-5B set lets any artist check whether their own images were scraped for Stable Diffusion/Midjourney and file a machine-readable opt-out — consent infrastructure shipped as a usable tool rather than a gallery metaphor; hundreds of millions of works were opted out within its first two years.

title: Igùn
venue_prize: The Shed, New York (2023–24); metaLAB (at) Harvard; Curatorial A(i)gents exhibition
decisive_move: Trains a StyleGAN exclusively on looted Benin Bronzes to generate what artisans might have produced during the 17-year artistic blackout that followed Britain's 1897 sacking of Benin — turning the restitution debate into a generative counter-history rather than a legal argument alone.

title: ImageNet Roulette
venue_prize: Released alongside 'Training Humans' exhibition, Fondazione Prada, Milan, 2019
decisive_move: Runs uploaded selfies through the raw, unfiltered ImageNet 'person' categories, surfacing the racist and misogynist labels baked into a foundational training set — went viral and forced ImageNet to purge over 600,000 photos.

title: In Notion: Dance Fiction
venue_prize: da:ns festival, Esplanade Singapore
decisive_move: Dancers' muscles are electrically re-stimulated from digitally recorded 'muscle memory' data captured from historic choreographers' bodies, blurring live performance and biometric playback until the audience cannot tell what is danced and what is data-driven.

title: Kudurru
venue_prize: Spawning (web platform)
decisive_move: A distributed network of member websites fingerprints and blocks AI scrapers in real time and, if a site opts in, can feed them corrupted data to poison the resulting model — consent enforcement flips from passive registry (opt-out lists) to active countermeasure.

title: Masked Reality
venue_prize: Art Bengaluru 2019; Leicester Art-AI Festival; Artissima Art Fair Turin 2021
decisive_move: Repurposes literal (modified) surveillance cameras to feed a custom-trained GAN that overwrites visitors' captured faces in real time with the ritual expressions of Kathakali and Theyyam performers, turning the apparatus of biometric capture into a vessel for endangered embodied heritage.

title: Mushroom Clouds
venue_prize: PHD Group, Hong Kong (Hong Kong Art Week 2026)
decisive_move: Builds a hand-made training dataset from 38 mushroom species found on Lantau Island, feeds it to a custom AI model, and exhibits the model's speculative new fungi beside a living terrarium — a small, sourced dataset set against LLM-scale data hunger.

title: Not the Only One (N'TOO)
venue_prize: Museum of Contemporary Photography Chicago; Seattle Art Fair; Carnegie Mellon University
decisive_move: Trains a sculptural voice-AI oracle exclusively on oral histories from three generations of the artist's own Black family, refusing commercial datasets she considers irreparably biased — the AI's glitches and refusals become deliberate pedagogy about data sovereignty rather than bugs to be fixed.

title: Once Upon A Garden (Once Upon a Flower series)
venue_prize: Commissioned by ARTX Lagos (2022); released via fellowship.xyz / daily.xyz
decisive_move: Builds her own GAN training sets from photographed Senegalese flowers and scanned national-archive material — rejecting biased large Western models — to speculatively generate flora that global warming and colonial archival gaps have already erased or never recorded.

title: Patchwork Girl
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2024 — Ars Electronica Award for Digital Humanity
decisive_move: A lecture performance built around an AI-generated avatar assembled from real online-harassment victims' testimonies, confronting audiences with synthesized, composite harm.

title: Pollinator Pathmaker
venue_prize: Eden Project; Serpentine Galleries; LAS Art Foundation; S+T+ARTS Prize
decisive_move: Encodes 'empathy for other species' as a literal algorithmic design parameter: a custom generative tool composes real, physically-planted gardens optimized for maximum pollinator-species diversity across a season, rather than for human visual preference - living, growing plants as the algorithm's actual output.

title: She Who Sees the Unknown
venue_prize: TRANSFER Gallery, New York; Eyebeam residency
decisive_move: 3D-scans and 3D-prints erased or demonized female/queer jinn figures from Middle Eastern mythology, pairing physical sculptures with an open digital archive and storytelling practice she names 'Re-Figuring' — coining and theorizing 'digital colonialism' as a critical framework for whose myths and data get to persist.

title: Source.Plus / PD12M
artist: Spawning (Mat Dryhurst, Holly Herndon, Jordan Meyer)
venue_prize: Spawning (source.plus)
decisive_move: Builds the rights-clear alternative to indiscriminate scraping: public-domain/CC0 images run through nudity, gore and PII classifiers and auto-recaptioned into PD12M, a 12.4-million-pair dataset — provenance-as-infrastructure, don't just flag stolen data, manufacture the clean substitute at training scale.

title: Starmirror
artist: Holly Herndon & Mat Dryhurst, with Sub
venue_prize: KW Institute for Contemporary Art, Berlin (31 Oct 2025 – 18 Jan 2026); K21, Kunstsammlung Nordrhein-Westfalen, Düsseldorf (27 Jun – 11 Oct 2026) — Kulturstiftung des Bundes 'Art & AI' programme
decisive_move: Trains a voice-cloning model on amateur choirs singing a commissioned songbook rooted in local traditions, turning audiences into willing, credited data-donors — a consensual, opt-in counter-model to nonconsensual voice-cloning.

title: Swell of spæc(i)es
venue_prize: Venice Biennale Arte 2024 (collateral event, commissioned by LAS Art Foundation), Accademia di Belle Arti di Venezia
decisive_move: Invites visitors to feed AI training sets beyond the Western canon — 3D-scanned West African deity statues, plankton, archival photographs — turning the gallery into a live intervention on whose data trains the machine.

title: Synthetic Memories
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2025 — Ars Electronica Award for Digital Humanity
decisive_move: Reconstructs fading or traumatic personal memories of elderly, dementia-affected, migrant and marginalized people into printed images via generative AI refined through live interviewee feedback loops.

title: The Call
venue_prize: Serpentine North, London (Serpentine Arts Technologies)
decisive_move: Recorded 15 UK community choirs under a purpose-written songbook/recording protocol, then placed the resulting voice dataset under a 'Data Trust' governance experiment (a Trusted Data Intermediary distributing power between contributors and model-users) before using it to train the choral AI models that fill the chapel-like installation — consent becomes both method and subject, not backstory.

title: The Finesse
artist: Christopher Kulendran Thomas, with Annika Kuhlmann
venue_prize: MCA Australia, Sydney — 'Data Dreams: Art and AI' (2025–26); originally KW Institute for Contemporary Art, Berlin
decisive_move: An AI continuously scrapes social media and re-edits the film live, weaving a Kardashian-like deepfake avatar into the defeated Tamil liberation movement's history — post-truth authorship as a never-repeating live installation.

title: The Island
venue_prize: Osservatorio Fondazione Prada, Milan
decisive_move: Stages an actor playing Flash Gordon 'appearing' on the real Croatian island of Korčula to save the world, threading documentary and fiction through sculptural projection-spheres — using 'junk time' vs. 'deep time' to interrogate what counts as real, staged, or generated footage in a media environment already saturated with synthetic images.

title: The Next Biennial Should Be Curated by a Machine
venue_prize: Commissioned by Liverpool Biennial & Whitney Museum of American Art (artport), hosted online
decisive_move: An AI trained on real Biennial and Whitney archives endlessly hallucinates fake artist biographies and press releases, exposing algorithmic curation's absurdity by actually letting a machine attempt it, live, forever.

title: The Sleight of the Machine
venue_prize: Plásmata 3 — Onassis Stegi, Pedion tou Areos, Athens
decisive_move: An AI video model is cast as a stage magician performing sleight-of-hand; rather than concealing the model's characteristic failures (hands multiplying, morphing, defying physics), the film keeps every glitch on screen as the actual 'trick' — generative failure turned into the evidentiary content.

title: V3: Model Collapse
venue_prize: The Photographers' Gallery, London (27 Jun-28 Sep 2025)
decisive_move: Third chapter of a four-part project in which each exhibition's own photographs and collages are fed back as training data for the next - literalizes AI model collapse as a visible, physically-installed material process, and draws an explicit parallel between data mining and geological mining extraction.

title: Venenosas, Nocivas e Suspeitas
venue_prize: Galeria de Fotos, Centro Cultural Fiesp, São Paulo (SESI-SP)
decisive_move: Three years of AI-generated fictional plants and portraits of erased women botanists expose the sexism and colonialism embedded in plant nomenclature; asked to depict aging women accurately, the AI systems balked at rendering wrinkles — their bias becoming part of the evidence on display.

title: xhairymutantx
venue_prize: Whitney Biennial 2024 'Even Better Than the Real Thing' (artport commission), Whitney Museum of American Art
decisive_move: Trains a text-to-image model on costumed, deliberately distorted images of Herndon's own body, so anyone prompting 'Holly Herndon' generates the artist's consented counter-likeness instead of an unauthorized deepfake — a first-person reclaiming of training-data consent.

title: 6,500 Alphabets Make a Map
venue_prize: transmediale 2026 – By the Mango Belt & Tamarind Road, Kuppelhalle, silent green Kulturquartier, Berlin
decisive_move: Large-scale dome installation built from 6,500 digitized African pictographs, syllabaries and ideographs (extending the artist's 2019–2023 'Ancestral Algorithm' project) that reframes 'algorithm' as 'algo + rhythm', proposing indigenous knowledge systems as an alternative computational epistemics rather than an ornament to it.

title: A Nation Is a Massacre
venue_prize: Pioneer Works, Brooklyn, 2019
decisive_move: A large-format text installation that lists/recites five centuries of massacres, missing and murdered Indigenous people, and environmental-racism incidents as a confrontational, register-like data litany covering a gallery floor and walls.

title: Ambiogenesis
venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
decisive_move: AI-voiced aquatic organisms narrate a post-human ecosystem with a 'radically different understanding of life and temporality,' using generative voice synthesis to give speculative, non-human timescales a first-person register.

title: Arachnid Orchestra. Jam Sessions
venue_prize: NTU Centre for Contemporary Art Singapore
decisive_move: Uses piezo pickups and laser Doppler vibrometers to capture real spider-web vibrations, then invites human musicians into live 'jam sessions' improvising alongside the arachnids' actual communication signals - an interspecies sonification instrument built from real vibrational data, not a metaphor for one.

title: Balot NFT / White Cube Lusanga
artist: CATPC (Congolese Plantation Workers Art League) with Human Activities / Renzo Martens
venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "Fellow Travellers" (Sept 21, 2024–2025); also S+T+ARTS Prize Africa '24 Grand Prize
decisive_move: Congolese ex-plantation workers mint NFTs of a contested 1931 anti-colonial sculpture ("Balot") held by a Virginia museum, using the sale proceeds to literally buy back plantation land — turning blockchain provenance into a restitution and land-reclamation instrument instead of a speculative art-market gimmick.

title: Barbed Wire Village
artist: Chao Tayiana Maina / Museum of British Colonialism
venue_prize: Baraza Media Lab, Nairobi
decisive_move: 3D-reconstructs suppressed British 'villagisation' and Mau Mau detention camps from archival and survivor data, rebuilding a physical Nairobi exhibition around a digitally modeled colonial crime scene that Kenya's post-independence archive had erased.

title: Breathing with the Forest
venue_prize: ACMI Melbourne, 'Works of Nature' (23 Nov 2023 - 14 Apr 2024)
decisive_move: Builds an architectural-scale, 3-channel immersive visualization around real carbon, water and mycorrhizal-network data drawn from a single Capinuri tree in the Colombian Amazon, making invisible more-than-human ecological reciprocity perceptible - though its sublime, cinematic staging leans closer to spectacle than to a data-justice argument.

title: Conversations with Bina48
venue_prize: Whitney Museum of American Art (acquired Fragments 7, 6, 5, 2, 2019)
decisive_move: Documents a years-long, ongoing videotaped dialogue between the artist and Bina48 — a social robot modeled on a Black woman but never programmed with Black history or identity — exposing the racial blind spots baked into 'universal' AI personhood.

title: Create Your Kampala
venue_prize: Hivos East Africa-supported campaign, Kampala, Uganda
decisive_move: Surveys 1,000 Kampala residents on failing public services (roads, water, waste, schools), then feeds that citizen dataset back into the city as public wall murals — a data report that returns as something walkable and communal instead of a locked PDF.

title: Cybernetics, or Ghosts?
artist: Michael Salu (editor) & James Ginzburg (music), with 15 international writers
venue_prize: CTM Festival 2025 – Stranger AI, radialsystem, Berlin (28 Jan 2025)
decisive_move: Fifteen writers respond in fiction to Italo Calvino's 1967 essay on cybernetics and myth, paired with a companion album, using the generative-AI moment as a mirror held up to older mythic and oral world-building traditions.

title: Data Justice Program & Digital Stewards
year: 2012–ongoing (Digital Stewards from 2012; Data Justice Program from 2016)
venue_prize: Detroit Community Technology Project / Detroit Digital Justice Coalition
decisive_move: Trains neighborhood residents as 'Digital Stewards' who physically build community-owned mesh Wi-Fi networks in Detroit neighborhoods, then extends that same infrastructural-sovereignty logic to the city's Open Data portal — resisting extraction by building parallel, community-held technical infrastructure rather than only critiquing the existing one.

title: Estados de Alerta (States of Alert)
venue_prize: 'Simbiología: Prácticas artísticas en un planeta en emergencia', Centro Cultural Kirchner (CCK), Buenos Aires
decisive_move: Three reactive robotic creatures — modeled on latex skin, goat, and cowhide morphologies — read a visitor's approach as threat and answer with self-preservation: sinuous skin tremors, a puff of charcoal dust, deployed steel spikes. The 'Other as threat to private property' is staged as animal instinct rather than human ideology.

title: Feedback VR, un musical antifuturista
venue_prize: IDFA DocLab Award for Immersive Non-Fiction 2025 (winner)
decisive_move: Peru's hi5-era 'sYmB0l$' internet slang is fused with Andean ritual sites inside a VR musical, reclaiming a decolonized digital vernacular instead of accepting a Silicon-Valley default aesthetic for what 'the future' of the internet is supposed to look like.

title: Forest Art Intelligence (FAI) / Analog Intelligence
venue_prize: TERN Samford Peri-urban SuperSite, Brisbane; ISEA2024 'Everywhen', Brisbane
decisive_move: Sensor-laced, 'lively materials' installations built directly into a regenerating forest ecosystem, treating the site's own ecological data as a co-author of the artwork rather than as a subject observed from outside.

title: Girjegumpi: The Sámi Architecture Library
venue_prize: Nordic Countries Pavilion, Venice Biennale — International Architecture Exhibition, 2023; sister collection at National Gallery of Canada
decisive_move: A nomadic, growing archive of 500+ books, artworks and objects on Sámi architecture and decoloniality, built as literal reindeer-herder-cabin infrastructure (and mirrored online as 'The Virtual Girjegumpi') rather than a representation of a library.

title: Give It Back
artist: New Red Order (Jackson Polys, Adam Khalil, Zack Khalil)
venue_prize: Artists Space, New York; touring galleries across US and Canada
decisive_move: Deadpan 'public secret society' videos and installations that document real, verifiable instances of land or money actually being returned to Indigenous nations, packaged as satirical settler-recruitment propaganda.

title: Indigenous Protocol and Artificial Intelligence Position Paper
artist: Jason Edward Lewis et al. (Indigenous Protocol and AI Working Group)
venue_prize: Indigenous Protocol and Artificial Intelligence Working Group / Concordia University (Spectrum repository)
decisive_move: Convenes Indigenous artists, scholars and technologists to draft founding ethical guidelines asking what AI would look like if it emerged from Indigenous rather than Western epistemologies — the canon document that seeded the later Abundant Intelligences research program.

title: Indigenous Protocols for AI — Gallery 3
artist: Angie Abdilla / Old Ways, New (with Jason Edward Lewis, Oiwi Parker Jones)
venue_prize: Old Ways, New — Gallery 3 (online); ANAT workshop series
decisive_move: Develops Indigenous-authored governance protocols ('Country Centred Design') for AI systems through cross-continental workshops (Aotearoa, Australia, North America, Pacific), presented as a public gallery of cultural output rather than a conventional single artwork — positioning First Nations peoples as authors of AI ethics, not subjects of it.

title: Lukautim Solwara (look out for the ocean) / Expanses
venue_prize: Biennale of Sydney; international presentations (Canada/Australia)
decisive_move: Performance-video works that re-embed queer, Indigenous Pacific (Sāmoan) bodies into colonial ocean and climate-change narratives, arguing that decolonising climate discourse itself is part of the artwork.

title: Mapu Kufüll (Land Seafood)
venue_prize: Museo del Hongo / FICWallmapu; touring screen program
decisive_move: Uses video-game-style 3D animation to follow a child gathering wild mushrooms under a grandmother's guidance, reclaiming fungi (mapu kufüll) — a food source that helped Mapuche communities survive displacement during the Occupation of Araucanía — as more-than-human ally and symbol of resistance, told through Indigenous rather than Western bio-art framing.

title: Masakhane
venue_prize: Founded at Deep Learning Indaba 2019; featured at Ars Electronica ('Who Owns the Truth')
decisive_move: A distributed, no-prerequisite community ('we build together' in isiZulu) crowdsources open datasets, translation and speech models for African languages historically excluded from big tech's language models — treating linguistic decolonization as ongoing collective infrastructure-building rather than a single authored work.

title: Matrix Vegetal
venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
decisive_move: A video installation braids Peruvian plant-healing practices together with 'quantum-inspired' technology, treating indeterminacy and non-Western epistemics of plant communication as equally valid registers of knowledge alongside computation.

title: Meditation on Country
artist: Angie Abdilla, with Ghillar Michael Anderson, Brian Schmidt and Katie Noon
venue_prize: MCA Australia, Sydney — 'Data Dreams: Art and AI' (21 Nov 2025 – 26 Apr 2026)
decisive_move: An immersive film installation uses machine learning to stage Aboriginal ancestral deep-time knowledge and Western astrophysics side by side under a simulated sky — refusing to let AI default to a single, Western epistemology of the cosmos.

title: MEMORY (Drawing Operations Unit: Generation 2 / D.O.U.G._2)
venue_prize: Victoria and Albert Museum, London (collection acquisition, 2022)
decisive_move: Performs live collaborative drawing alongside a robotic arm driven by a recurrent neural network trained on two decades of her own gestures; the V&A later acquires the trained model itself — sealed in a slowly degrading 3D-printed resin case — the first AI model collected as an artwork by a major museum, staging human/machine co-authorship as more-than-human kinship rather than tool-use.

title: Myconnect
artist: Saša Spačal, Mirjan Švagelj, Anil Podgornik
year: 2013 (Honorary Mention, Prix Ars Electronica 2015; Prix Cube 2016 nominee)
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2015 (Honorary Mention); exhibited in Spain, Germany, Russia, Serbia, Greece
decisive_move: A participant's heartbeat is converted to an electrical signal and fed live into oyster/shiitake mycelium cultures in petri dishes; the mycelium's bioelectric resistance response is captured and converted back into vibration, sound and light for the participant - a literal two-way 'mycosynapse' between human vital signs and a living fungal network, questioning the anthropocentric division of nature-human-technology.

title: Mythmachine
venue_prize: BALTIC Centre for Contemporary Art, Gateshead (commission, 'play' strand)
decisive_move: An AI-driven virtual biome of post-human creatures listens to and choreographs itself from ambient audio feedback captured live in the gallery, proposing folklore-derived 'other' intelligences as an alternative to extractive machine learning.

title: Nepenthe (Summer Palace Ruins Edition)
venue_prize: QUAD, Derby, UK; 24th Biennale of Sydney, White Bay Power Station (2024)
decisive_move: Digitally rebuilds the looted, burned ruins of the Yuanmingyuan Summer Palace as a walkable open-world game space, turning a colonial wound into explorable virtual terrain and raising the question of virtual restitution.

title: netart_latino database
venue_prize: Self-published, netart.org.uy/latino/ (Uruguay)
decisive_move: A hand-coded, ASCII-mapped database indexes Latin American net artists by national domain (.mx, .ve, .uy...) instead of Northern-institution categories — an inverted cartography that makes the region a creative center rather than a periphery importing art from elsewhere.

title: Night Fishing with Ancestors
venue_prize: Secession, Vienna (2023); also Mori Art Museum Tokyo, Goldsmiths CCA London
decisive_move: An Indigenous-run collective of roughly 30 Belyuen community members re-stages 18th-century Macassan–Aboriginal trade history using iPhone-shot 'improvisational realism,' layering and superimposing footage to collapse colonial linear time into simultaneous ancestral and contemporary registers.

title: No conciliation is possible (working diagram)
venue_prize: Liverpool Biennial 2023, Tate Liverpool
decisive_move: A site-specific wall-diagram installation (map-like drawings, objects, annotations) that treats decolonization as unfinished, still-computing political data rather than a settled historical fact — the diagram-as-system is the artwork, not a single image.

title: Nosukaay
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2024 — Interactive Art+ (Golden Nica)
decisive_move: A hand-woven West African (Manjak) loincloth functions as a literal keyboard/interface, arguing that weaving and computing share a common ancestral logic — an alternative, non-Western origin story for the machine.

title: Our Data Bodies / Digital Defense Playbook
artist: Our Data Bodies Project (Detroit Community Technology Project, Stop LAPD Spying Coalition, Center for Community Transitions)
venue_prize: Allied Media Projects (publisher); odbproject.org
decisive_move: Runs two years of workshops in Detroit, Charlotte and LA neighborhoods so residents can map how re-entry, housing and welfare data systems police them, then distills the findings into a free community 'Digital Defense Playbook' of counter-surveillance tools instead of an academic report.

title: overturns
venue_prize: Swiss Institute, New York (first US institutional solo exhibition)
decisive_move: Reworks Sylvia Wynter's call to overturn Western 'genres of the human' into a suite of diagrams, models and sculptural systems mapping planetary racial capital against co-existing Black and Indigenous cosmologies — treating decolonization as a systems/data problem, not a site-specific one.

title: Paradise Camp
venue_prize: Aotearoa New Zealand Pavilion, Venice Biennale 2022 (curated by Natalie King)
decisive_move: Restages Gauguin's Sāmoa-derived colonial-ethnographic paintings as tableau photographs with a Fa'afafine cast, building a counter-archive ('Vārchive') that exposes the source images while entangling colonisation, gender and climate catastrophe.

title: Phyto-Travellers
venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — ZKM_Kubus Subraum, part of "Fellow Travellers" (July 26, 2025–Feb 22, 2026)
decisive_move: A living garden of colonial-era "neophyte" plants is grown on freight pallets inside a 2:3-scale reconstruction of Columbus's ship the Niña, with the plants' original indigenous names inscribed on the glass façade — a literal, growing archive of botanical colonialism rather than a data visualization of it.

title: PL'AI
venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
decisive_move: An AI-controlled robot 'plays' with live cucumber plants in a terrarium via Lidar scanning and colored-ball placement, forcing a literal, physical protocol of interspecies communication between machine intelligence and plant behavior rather than a metaphorical one.

title: Primate Visions: Macaque Macabre
venue_prize: Museum MACAN, Jakarta (solo exhibition)
decisive_move: Turns Sulawesi crested macaques — framed by colonial primatology as lesser life — into protagonists of a camp horror-theatre cosmology drawn from Minahasan belief, granting agency to more-than-human entities against anthropocentric state modernity.

title: Pȟehíŋ kiŋ líla akhíšoke (Her hair was heavy)
venue_prize: SAW Video / Knot Project Space; artist portfolio
decisive_move: Performs wearing a braided-hair interface woven with sensors and machine learning that 'listens without ears,' translating touch and movement into light, sound and AI-generated text — staging the technology as a spirit-greeting device within Lakȟóta protocol rather than a neutral tool.

title: Repatriate (and broader climate-performance practice)
venue_prize: Performance, Australian contemporary performance circuit (Campbelltown Arts Centre and touring)
decisive_move: The Tongan-Australian artist's own body performs an endurance ritual — dancing a Tongan liturgical choreography inside a Perspex tank slowly filled by fire hydrants over 90 minutes — as a direct physical proxy for Pacific sea-level-rise data.

title: Repellent Fence
venue_prize: US/Mexico border, Douglas AZ–Agua Prieta MX (four-day land art action)
decisive_move: Floats 26 ten-foot 'scare-eye' balloons, decorated with Indigenous medicine colors, in a two-mile line straddling the US/Mexico border — a temporary, four-day land-art 'suture' re-joining Indigenous land that a colonial border fence had divided.

title: SummitShare — Open Digital Infrastructure for Shared Histories (Cultural Heritage)
artist: SummitShare / Shared Histories Team (Mulenga Kapwepwe, Thomas Gondwe, Nhyira Amofa-Sekyi, Mario Jere)
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Golden Nica)
decisive_move: A blockchain-based open-access virtual museum performing 'metadata healing' on colonial-era Zambian artifacts, centering indigenous women custodians and routing financial support back to heritage communities.

title: Takapau
artist: Mataaho Collective (Erena Baker, Sarah Hudson, Bridget Reweti, Terri Te Tau)
venue_prize: Venice Biennale, International Exhibition 'Stranieri Ovunque – Foreigners Everywhere' — Golden Lion for Best Participant, 2024
decisive_move: Monumental woven/lashed hi-vis tie-down installation that scales a domestic, matrilineal Māori fibre-craft practice — mat-weaving used in childbirth ceremony — up to architectural size, forming the Arsenale's entry passage.

title: The Kris Project (I–III)
venue_prize: Singapore Art Museum collection; finalist, APB Foundation Signature Art Prize 2018
decisive_move: Splices found footage from 1950s–60s Cathay-Keris Malayan cinema with wartime documentary material through an invented fictional filmmaker persona, collapsing fact and propaganda to expose whose version of Malayan history counts as 'archival record.'

title: The Open Boat
venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "Fellow Travellers" opening performance, Sept 20, 2024
decisive_move: A sound/performance piece built from postcolonial theory and the non-linear, cyclical temporalities of oceanic space, staging Black Atlantic migration and diaspora as a counter-epistemology to Western linear data and archival logic.

title: The Planetary Compendium
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Honorary Mention)
decisive_move: A living, continuously evolving digital atlas of essays and case studies mapping planetary-governance frameworks, built as a scalable educational tool rather than a fixed artwork.

title: The REDress Project
venue_prize: Traveling public installation; permanent display, Canadian Museum for Human Rights, Winnipeg; shown at National Museum of the American Indian, Washington DC
decisive_move: Empty red dresses (400+, many donated by families) hung in public sites across North America to make the uncounted statistical gap of missing and murdered Indigenous women viscerally, spatially present as absence.

title: Three Experiments in Plant Intercognition
venue_prize: National Institute of Chemistry, Ljubljana
decisive_move: Stages literal cognitive-communication experiments between artist and germinating plants (extending her 'Skotopoiesis' bio-semiotic method), treating a plant's measurable response to light and stimuli as a form of intelligence to test the human-drawn boundaries of cognition.

title: Transfiguraciones
venue_prize: (de)Stabilizing Diffusions — MUTEK Montréal x Machine Agencies (Concordia) x SAT
decisive_move: A GAN trained on digitized datasets of Prehispanic Mesoamerican masks is played live like an instrument, its outputs morphing in real time under the performers' control — the neural network treated as a possessed object to be exorcised/decolonized rather than a neutral generative tool.

title: Uncharted
venue_prize: Tribeca Immersive 2025 ("In Search of Us")
decisive_move: 6,500 characters collected from African and diasporic writing systems are rendered as an AI-driven data sculpture that a dancer must physically interpret in real time — an endangered-language archive made legible only through embodied performance, not a screen or a search bar.

title: Yanmeeyarr
venue_prize: ACMI (Australian Centre for the Moving Image), Melbourne — permanent commission through 2031
decisive_move: Ochre body-marking patterns used in Gunditjmara ceremony, translated into a light-and-shadow installation across the museum's ceiling, framing the threshold visitors cross into and out of a history of the moving image.

title: AI, Ain't I a Woman?
venue_prize: Algorithmic Justice League / MIT
