decisive_move: A spoken-word video poem restages Sojourner Truth's 1851 question through five commercial facial-analysis APIs (Microsoft, IBM, Amazon, Face++, Google) misgendering Oprah, Serena Williams, Michelle Obama and Shirley Chisholm on screen — turns benchmark error rates into a recited indictment.

title: Algorithmic Justice League
venue_prize: ajl.org (nonprofit, est. 2016)
decisive_move: Institutionalizes 'the coded gaze' as a named, citable object of critique — fuses art, research and policy testimony into a standing instrument that keeps auditing deployed AI systems rather than resting on a single artwork.

title: Coded Bias
artist: Shalini Kantayya (director), featuring Joy Buolamwini, Cathy O'Neil, Zeynep Tufekci, Safiya Noble, Deborah Raji
venue_prize: Sundance Film Festival 2020; Netflix / PBS Independent Lens
decisive_move: Documents the Gender Shades audit escalating into congressional testimony and municipal facial-recognition bans, narrativizing an algorithmic accuracy study as a feminist accountability thriller.

title: Data Feminism
venue_prize: MIT Press (open access edition)
decisive_move: Reframes data science through intersectional feminist theory, arguing that counting and classification themselves encode power — turns 'whose voice is missing from the data' into a repeatable analytic method rather than a moral aside.

title: Dear Data
venue_prize: MoMA permanent collection (postcards + sketchbooks)
decisive_move: Two designers mail each other one hand-drawn postcard a week for a year, each encoding a mundane personal metric (complaints, doors touched, laughter) — asserts 'data humanism': the antidote to extractive big data is slow, subjective, handmade small data shared as intimacy.

title: Decoding Bias
venue_prize: Lumen Prize 2024, Gold Award (overall winner)
decisive_move: Eight hyper-realistic AI avatars are staged in a self-run group-therapy circle to 'purge' their own biased training data and toxic code — algorithmic bias literalized as a confession the machines are made to perform in front of a live audience.

title: Feminist Data Set
venue_prize: Ars Electronica, LABoral, Victoria and Albert Museum, MAMbo Bologna, SOHO20 Gallery
decisive_move: Interrogates every stage of a machine-learning pipeline — collection, labeling, training, algorithm choice, chatbot design — through participatory feminist workshops, treating deliberately slow, intentional data-gathering itself as an act of protest against extractive data culture.

title: Gender Shades
venue_prize: MIT Media Lab; published at Conference on Fairness, Accountability and Transparency (FAccT)
decisive_move: Builds an intersectional benchmark (Pilot Parliaments Benchmark, 1,270 faces) to show commercial gender-classifiers erring up to 34.7% on darker-skinned women versus 0.8% on lighter-skinned men — an academic audit engineered as public evidence, which pushed IBM to end facial-recognition sales.

title: Harvesting Climate Action
venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Honorary Mention)
decisive_move: Combines digital sensing tools with community culinary-identity workshops in a three-tiered (individual/group/district) structure to build climate-data awareness from the ground up.
curator_note: Schwacher KI-Bezug — partizipative Sensorik + Community-Workshops; als Daten-Partizipations-Werk geführt.

title: Here the River Lies
venue_prize: Singapore Art Museum
decisive_move: Crowdsources 1,500+ oral micro-histories of a vanished river onto a hand-drawn map and deliberately refuses to distinguish verified memory from invented story, indexing both into the same archive as a challenge to the evidentiary claims of official heritage data.

title: Minotauros (Minotaurs)
venue_prize: Big Bang Data (curated by Olga Subirós & José Luis de Vicente), Centro de Cultura Digital, Mexico City
decisive_move: An 8-bit-aesthetic LED labyrinth animates Mexico City's informal-economy occupation rate — rendering, in low-resolution video-game imagery, the workers who accumulate no data trail toward a pension: missing data made visibly to move.

title: Missing Datasets (list/essay)
venue_prize: Self-published open repository, companion to the Library of Missing Datasets installation series
decisive_move: An open, continually-added public list plus essay cataloguing real-world datasets that go uncollected, giving the physical Library's concept a living, crowd-legible digital counterpart.

title: Shifting Sands: A Battle Song
venue_prize: Saudi Arabia Pavilion, 60th Venice Biennale
decisive_move: Gathers texts, drawings, and voice recordings from over 1,000 Saudi women across nationwide workshops into a single immersive multimedia installation, converting collected first-person testimony into a national-pavilion-scale statistical portrait of women's status.

title: Sobrevivientes
artist: Datasketch (with Sandra Suárez / QueremosDatos.co, Data Against Feminicide network)
venue_prize: Datasketch, Bogotá, Colombia — 'Data Against Feminicide' international network
decisive_move: Denied complete femicide records through freedom-of-information requests, the team built its own database from news reports and survivor testimony, then translated the gaps into embroidery, illustration, digital maps, and a public-space intervention — data activism that makes the state's non-counting visible.

title: Stealth Care: Wellness from the Algorithm
venue_prize: ISEA2024 (29th International Symposium on Electronic Art), Brisbane — presented at Outer Space
decisive_move: The artist's own chronically-ill, 'non-normative' muscle data, captured via a wearable EMG device over six months of research with Ars Electronica x IT:U, is fed into ML models built for wellness/health platforms — exposing whose bodies the quantified-self industry was never built to include.

title: The Library of Missing Datasets
venue_prize: bitforms gallery (v2.0); developed during residencies at Data & Society and Eyebeam
decisive_move: A literal filing cabinet of empty, labeled folders — each naming a dataset that societies choose not to collect (e.g. undocumented migrants incarcerated in the US) — makes structural absence itself the exhibit.

title: The Library of Missing Datasets (v2.0)
venue_prize: bitforms gallery, New York
decisive_move: Fills steel filing cabinets with empty folders labeled by the names of datasets that structurally do not exist ('People excluded from housing due to criminal records', 'Uncounted deaths tied to Hurricane Maria') — making data's absence itself the exhibit, by a Nigerian-American artist working at the intersection of data collection and racial/social omission.

title: un- (anterior ellipse[s] as mangled container; or where edges meet to wedge and [un]moor)
venue_prize: Whitney Biennial 2024 'Even Better Than the Real Thing', Whitney Museum of American Art
decisive_move: Six panes of dark glass suspended at a threatening angle over visitors' heads, restaging the 1964 CORE 'stall-in' protest as a physical mechanics of surveillance and Black refusal — power made into a literal ceiling.

title: Vigil
venue_prize: Talking Stick Festival, Vancouver, 2002 (Gore and Cordova Streets, Downtown Eastside)
decisive_move: A durational street performance — scrubbing the pavement, yelling the first names of real missing women written on her arms, then nailing a red dress to a pole until it tears — marking the unresolved case count of over 65 missing Downtown Eastside women.

title: The Feral: Epoch 1
venue_prize: MCA Australia, Sydney — 'Data Dreams: Art and AI' (2025–26)
decisive_move: First chapter of a planned 1,000-year, 32-generation project in which successive artists 'feed' one AI so it can eventually make a film witnessing humanity's end — a deliberately glacial experiment in what a machine 'learns' across centuries rather than scraped-in-seconds data.

title: Thousand Lives (BOB lineage)
venue_prize: Gladstone Gallery, Seoul; Pilar Corrias, London; Matadero Madrid
decisive_move: Extends Cheng's neurosymbolic autonomous-agent architecture (originally built for BOB, 2018-19) into a live, indefinitely-running simulation of a pet turtle's daily belief-updating - an actually-executing inferential AI system exhibited as artwork rather than a fixed narrative or render.

title: ASSEMBLY
venue_prize: Venice Biennale, Australia Pavilion, 2019
decisive_move: Traces the Michela stenographic keyboard used for real Italian and Australian parliamentary record-keeping, following its data as it is translated across written word, code, music and choreography, filmed inside actual Senate chambers.

title: Climate temperature-anomaly sonification
venue_prize: YouTube / Forbes coverage, using Berkeley Earth data
decisive_move: Converts global land-based temperature anomalies since 1880 (Berkeley Earth dataset) into one musical note per year against a 1950-1980 baseline, making 150 years of warming audible as an accelerating, increasingly dissonant climb rather than a chart.

title: data-verse (trilogy)
venue_prize: High Museum of Art, Atlanta; Amos Rex Helsinki; Copenhagen Contemporary; Almine Rech
decisive_move: Renders CERN/NASA-scale scientific and cosmological datasets as monumental sound-and-light spectacle - pure data-sublime awe with no explicit interpretive critique of AI or power. Included deliberately as the data-sublime contrast case named in the brief.

title: DATALAND
artist: Refik Anadol Studio (Refik Anadol & Efsun Erkılıç)
venue_prize: The Grand LA, Los Angeles ('world's first museum of AI art')
decisive_move: A permanent 25,000 sq ft museum where a wristband ('Empathica') and scent-diffusing neckband feed anonymized visitor biometric/behavioral signals back into generative rainforest-data visuals — data spectacle fully institutionalized as a standing museum genre rather than a temporary blockbuster stunt.

title: teamLab Borderless
venue_prize: teamLab Borderless, Azabudai Hills, Tokyo
decisive_move: Included deliberately as the spectacle-pole counterweight: real-time motion-tracking and projection-mapped generative visuals produce pure immersive wonder with no explicit interrogation of data, AI, or power — the mirror image of every investigation-pole work in this set.

title: The Street is Watching You (Face the Vitrine)
venue_prize: D-CAF — Downtown Contemporary Arts Festival, Cairo
decisive_move: Projects giant tracking eyes onto a downtown Cairo storefront so passersby physically feel themselves being watched in real time — literalizing street harassment and the CCTV/state gaze as a shared bodily experience instead of an abstract policy debate.

title: Unsupervised
venue_prize: MoMA, New York (Gund Lobby; acquired into permanent collection)
decisive_move: Runs a generative model over 138,151 digitized works from MoMA's own collection and streams the output as a continuously mutating real-time 'hallucination' on a 24×24-ft media wall — no argument, no disclosed critique of the compute/energy behind it; over 3 million visitors, pure data-sublime as the point.
